# Immanentize

> Stephen Bourke, co-founder of Echo (acquired by McKesson in 2021). Operator-advisor to healthtech founders and their investors. Based in London.

## About Stephen Bourke

Stephen Bourke is a British healthtech operator and advisor based in London.

- Co-founded Echo in 2015, which became the UK's biggest digital pharmacy.
- Scaled Echo to 500,000 patients, $120m+ ARR and 300 employees.
- Led the sale of Echo to McKesson in 2021. The company now trades as LloydsDirect.
- Before Echo: General Manager of LloydsPharmacy Online Doctor at McKesson (2012–2015). Launched the service in Ireland and Australia.
- Before McKesson: Head of Strategy at BT Business (2008–2012).
- NHS Innovation Fellow (2019–2022).
- Board positions: Leva / Cellen (pain clinic, NED); Royal College of GPs Enterprise (former NED); Kearney FFWD advisory circle; University of Oxford Department of Engineering (advisor).
- Angel investor in European healthtech and SaaS.
- MSc, Marketing Management, Vlerick Business School (Ghent). BA (Hons), Economics and Philosophy, University College Dublin. Year at Université Paris-Sorbonne.

## About Immanentize

Immanentize is Stephen Bourke's operator-advisory practice.

- Founded 2021. Based in London.
- Advises healthtech founders and investors.
- Scope: commercial strategy, pricing, partnerships, growth, international expansion, and the commercial side of M&A. Also operator-partner work for private equity and post-deal 100-day plans.
- Engagement models: short diagnostics (2–4 weeks) or longer engagements (3–6 months, part-time, inside the team).
- Work globally, mostly in Europe.
- Confidential by default. No workshops, no slideware. Measured in outcomes and P&L.

## Team

Stephen leads. He works with a small bench of trusted ex-founders, clinicians, scientists, lawyers and operators whom he has known for years. Specialisms range from setting up third-party logistics to reviewing academic papers.

## Recognition

- KPMG Best British Mobile Start-Up — Winner (Echo)
- Mayor of London's Outstanding Achievement — Winner (Echo)
- Accenture HealthTech Innovation Challenge — Global Finalist
- Great British Entrepreneur Awards — UK Finalist
- The Spectator Economic Innovator of the Year — UK Finalist

## Press

- Echo acquired by McKesson (TechCrunch, February 2021): https://techcrunch.com/2021/02/18/lloydspharmacy-owner-mckesson-acquires-uk-online-pharmacy-startup-echo/
